title: 'Better get smart soon!' Trump tells Iran to 'get their act together'; shares AI image with a gun
content: President Trump criticized Iran's negotiation tactics, stating they "don't know how to sign a non-nuclear deal" and urged them to "get smart soon." This comes as the US reportedly prepares for an extended blockade strategy to limit Iran's regional influence, shifting from short-term measures to sustained economic and military pressure.

title: 'Historic hat-trick of victories': PM Modi hails 'fearless voting in Bengal'
content: Prime Minister Modi lauded West Bengal's high voter turnout in the second phase of assembly elections, highlighting a "fearless atmosphere." He expressed confidence in a BJP hat-trick of victories across five states, citing recent successes in Bihar and Gujarat. The results on May 4 are anticipated to bolster the resolve for a developed India.

title: Keymer Exclusive: Being German No. 1, Magnus intimidation, and more
content: In this exclusive interview, Vincent Keymer reflected on his rise from winning the Grenke Open at 13 to claiming the Grenke Freestyle 2026 title in Karlsruhe. Germany’s No. 1 spoke about freestyle chess relying more on intuition, handling the “intimidation factor” of Magnus Carlsen, and balancing school with chess while growing up in a family of musicians. Keymer also praised coach Péter Lékó and discussed the challenges young chess players face in Germany compared to countries like India.

title: Google withdraws from Pentagon's $100 million drone contest
content: Google quietly withdrew from a Pentagon competition to build voice-controlled autonomous drone swarms—after initially being selected as a successful submission. The $100 million contest, run by Special Operations Command and the Defense Innovation Unit, seeks technology to direct drone swarms in real-time battlefield scenarios. Google cited "resourcing" issues, but the exit is said to be  followed by an internal ethics review. OpenAI, Palantir, and xAI remain in the running.

title: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, the cheat code batter - Even Bumrah & Co. have nowhere to hide
content: At just fifteen, Vaibhav Sooryavanshi is setting the IPL 2026 alight with his breathtaking batting skills, reminiscent of legends like Sachin Tendulkar. His audacious style against elite bowlers, boasting strike rates often soaring past 200, challenges every cricketing norm. In Sooryavanshi’s case, he has aced that test like no other. Almost like using the cheat codes in EA Sports Cricket 07.

title: Why a gunman at the WHCD epitomises American idiosyncrasy
content: Jean Paul-Sartre once said: Freedom is what you do with what’s been done to you, and America – with its freedom from Britain and other colonial powers – develop its own peculiarities, one of which is the White House Correspondents’ Dinner, a night where the press and the White House ostensibly bury their differences over a few shots. Normally those shots are jokes like Obama’s anger translator or Reagan dialling in after an assassination attempt, but this year they came from the barrel of a deranged gunman who had managed to waltz past security.

title: After $6.25bn 'gift' to 'Trump accounts', Michael & Susan Dell donate $750m
content: Michael and Susan Dell have donated $750 million to the University of Texas at Austin for a new medical center and research campus. This facility will integrate AI and advanced computing to improve patient care, research, and education, with construction beginning this year and opening by 2030. The donation builds on their extensive philanthropic history and commitment to Austin's growth.

title: PM Modi inaugurates UP's longest expressway; check travel time, route, speed limit
content: One of the most noticeable benefits of Ganga Expressway will be the sharp reduction in travel time. The trip between Meerut and Prayagraj, which currently takes around 10 to 12 hours, is likely to be cut to approximately 6 to 7 hours.

title: 'Dhurandhar 2' collection: Ranveer Singh starrer eyes shattering 'Baahubali 2' record
content: 'Dhurandhar 2: The Revenge', directed by Aditya Dhar and starring Ranveer Singh, Sanjay Dutt, Arjun Rampal, R. Madhavan, Rakesh Bedi, Sara Arjun and Yami Gautam in a cameo, is just Rs. 7.24 crore away from surpassing 'Baahubali 2: The Conclusion's nine-year worldwide record of Rs. 1,788.06 crore to become the second highest-grossing Indian film of all time globally after 'Dangal'. On Day 42, the film's worldwide gross stands at Rs. 1,780.82 crore with an India net of Rs. 1,133.05 crore, approximately Rs. 101 crore away from 'Pushpa 2: The Rule's all-time domestic record of Rs. 1,234.1 crore. Released across 10,000 screens on March 19, 2026 with a record Rs. 90 crore opening day, the 229-minute U/A certified espionage thriller has achieved all of this without a single show in China or the six Gulf markets where it remains banned.

title: White House gets a 'rethink' after Donald Trump said Anthropic was 'shaping up'
content: The White House is reportedly considering easing restrictions on AI firm Anthropic, a shift from its previous 'supply chain risk' label. This comes after President Trump's positive remarks and a meeting with Anthropic's CEO.

title: Babar breaks massive T20 record; joins Kohli, Abhishek in elite list
content: Babar Azam's spectacular century propelled Peshawar Zalmi to the PSL final, defeating Islamabad United. His unbeaten 103 powered Zalmi to 221, a total too daunting for United. This remarkable innings marked Babar's fourth PSL century, equalling a record, and his ninth T20 hundred as captain, a new global benchmark.

title: Ghaziabad family’s ‘destination wedding’ at Almora cremation ground sparks massive row
content: A riverside cremation site in Uttarakhand's Almora district was used for a destination wedding, sparking outrage and prompting an administration probe. The ceremony, which involved a couple from Ghaziabad and around 50 guests, took place on April 25 at a ghat traditionally used for last rites.

title: Mass resignations rock Sri Lanka Cricket as prez, executive committee quit
content: Shammi Silva resigned as president of Sri Lanka Cricket along with the board’s entire executive committee, the organisation confirmed on Wednesday. Silva had led SLC since 2019, overseeing Asia Cup successes but also disappointing World Cup campaigns and declining rankings for the men’s team. The reason for the mass resignations has not been officially disclosed.

title: West Bengal polls: Burdwan homes go without help as maid gets BJP ticket
content: In a devastating turn of events, two cousins aged 20 and 14 were killed in a hit-and-run accident on Ashoka Road in central Delhi, just after enjoying an IPL match. The massive truck, which turned recklessly at the Mahadev Road T-point, crushed the young duo on their motorbike.

title: From Riyan Parag to ABD: Cricketers seen vaping on camera
content: Rajasthan Royals captain Riyan Parag is in hot water after visuals of him vaping in the dressing room during an IPL 2026 match went viral. This incident raises serious concerns as vaping is banned in India. It adds to a string of player conduct controversies in the league.  The league faces renewed scrutiny over player behavior and rule enforcement.

title: Riddhi Dogra recalls suffering from ‘anxiety’ while playing Shah Rukh Khan’s mother in Jawan
content: Riddhi Dogra, a recent fan-favorite from 'The 50', shared her surreal experience playing Shah Rukh Khan's mother in 'Jawan'. She revealed her childhood admiration for SRK fueled her decision, despite initial anxieties about portraying an older role. Director Atlee's team struggled to age her, ultimately embracing her youthful appearance as a deliberate narrative choice for the film's flashback sequences.

title: Sonam Raghuvanshi gets bail in 4th attempt over 'pre-arrest lapses'; what happened during court hearing
content: The recent bail granted to Sonam Raghuvanshi by a local court has left many in disbelief, especially in light of accusations that she planned her husband’s murder during their honeymoon. The decision, hinged on perceived missteps in the arrest notification, has led to public outcry from the victim's family.

title: 'No vacuum in law to deal with the offence' — What Supreme Court said on hate speeches
content: The Supreme Court ruled that current laws adequately address hate speech, stating punishment is a legislative matter and no vacuum exists. However, it encouraged the government to assess if new legislation is needed due to changing social dynamics.

title: Watch: 108 Ambulance soiled by accident victim, staffer takes money, tells wife to clean it
content: A 108 ambulance staffer in Katni district allegedly extorted money from a critically injured road accident victim's family and forced a female relative to clean the ambulance. The disturbing incident, captured on video, has led to an official inquiry and a notice issued to the staffer for an explanation.

title: 'Same aura': 15-year-old Sooryavanshi compared to Dhoni, Tendulkar
content: At just 15, Vaibhav Sooryavanshi has become one of the IPL’s biggest attractions with his fearless batting and powerful strokeplay. Former India pacer Mohit Sharma compared the youngster’s nationwide popularity and “aura” to MS Dhoni, saying he has seen only two players command such admiration. Ex-India cricketer Murali Karthik also hailed Sooryavanshi as a “genuine prodigy”, comparing his rise and fame to that of Sachin Tendulkar.

title: Gujarat local body election: Hindu woman scripts victory in Muslim-majority Godhra ward
content: In a remarkable display of unity, independent candidate Apexa Soni secured a win in Godhra's communally sensitive Ward 7, a locality with minimal Hindu voters. Residents, primarily from the Muslim community, rallied behind Soni, who had consistently addressed local civic issues even after a narrow loss in 2021. Her victory highlights people transcending divisions for a dedicated public servant.

title: ‘It’s an arduous task': Iyer on growing crisis for bowlers after loss
content: Punjab Kings captain Shreyas Iyer lamented bowling execution after their first IPL 2026 loss. Despite a strong 224, the team couldn't contain Rajasthan Royals' late surge, particularly from Ferreira and Dubey. Iyer acknowledged the challenge for bowlers in modern T20s but stressed execution is key. He remains optimistic, calling it a learning experience.

title: Ghaziabad apartment fire: Massive blaze erupts in Indirapuram, multiple flats gutted
content: A massive fire erupted at Gaur Green Avenue society in Indirapuram on Wednesday morning, gutting an estimated 10 to 12 flats and causing panic among residents. Fire tenders responded swiftly to the blaze, which is suspected to have been triggered by an electrical fault. Dousing operations are underway to prevent further spread.

title: Is Trump getting the full picture? Vance raises concerns over Iran war
content: US Vice President J D Vance is questioning the Pentagon's assessment of the Iran conflict, concerned about undisclosed missile stockpile depletion. He fears this could impact responses to global flashpoints like Taiwan, South Korea, and Europe.  Despite official assurances, some believe the situation is being downplayed, potentially affecting future military readiness.

title: Riyan Parag captured vaping inside dressing room during IPL match
content: Rajasthan Royals captain Riyan Parag was filmed vaping in the team's dressing room during a live broadcast, sparking controversy. The act is illegal in India under the Prohibition of Electronic Cigarettes Act, 2019, raising concerns about athlete health and regulatory compliance. This incident follows a previous breach of protocol by the RR manager.

title: The Phase 2 trust vote in West Bengal: Who will win the Matua votes?
content: Electoral analysts estimate that Matua voters influence outcomes in 60 to 65 assembly seats. In the second phase alone, they are a key factor in 21 constituencies. In tightly contested elections, this scale of influence gives the community leverage that political parties cannot ignore. This is evident in recent electoral trends.

title: Lizard in paneer tikka? Bengaluru cabbie hospitalised after bakery visit
content: A man in Bengaluru was hospitalized after allegedly finding a lizard in his paneer tikka roll from a local bakery. He experienced vomiting and diarrhea, leading to a 24-hour observation period. Police have registered a case, though the complainant could not provide photographic evidence of the alleged contamination.

title: IPL 2026: For batters, struggle is real in slightly tough conditions
content: Having Been Spoon-Fed T20 Belters, They Have Been Unable To Adapt When Needed Most. Should Indian Cricket Be Worried About Success Away From Home?

title: Bengal phase 2 polls: TMC worker attacked, BJP claims voter booth vandalised
content: West Bengal's second phase of Assembly elections commenced amidst reports of violence, voter intimidation, and technical issues. Incidents were reported across Chapra, Shantipur, Nimtala, and Bhangar. Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee alleged bias from observers and TMC party flags being removed, while the BJP reported an assault on a polling agent in Chapra.

title: Meghalaya 'honeymoon murder' case: Raja Raghuvanshi’s family shocked after Sonam gets bail
content: The family of slain Indore businessman Raja Raghuvanshi is demanding a CBI probe after his wife, a prime accused in his murder, was granted bail by a Meghalaya court. Raja's mutilated body was found in June last year, and his wife, along with others, was arrested.

title: Watch: 6 wickets in 6 balls! Cricketer scripts history with bowling
content: Myles Davis made cricket history by taking six wickets in six balls for Penkridge Cricket Club against Pelsall Cricket Club in the South Staffordshire County League. Davis finished with figures of 7-16 and described the achievement as “surreal” and “amazing”, with the rare double hat-trick believed to be only the seventh such feat worldwide.

title: Global giant Heineken’s GCC in Hyderabad inaugurated
content: It is expected to create over 300 high-value jobs in the initial phase, with employment projected to scale up to 1,600 in the coming years, says Telangana IT and Industries Minister’s office.

title: Law permits Perarivalan to practise as lawyer: T.N. Bar Council enrolment committee chairman
content: K. Balu says Section 24A of the Advocates Act permits former convicts to enrol as lawyers after a cooling-off period of two years pursuant to their release from prison

title: Siberian Huskies abandoned on Hyderabad outskirts, found severely dehydrated; one dead, eight traced
content: The dogs, aged between 2-3 years, were left in open areas far from habitation, with no access to shade or water during peak summer conditions

title: No rest for the wicked: Even as Tamil Nadu comes down from its election high, tensions are still rising
content: The issues vary from the advisability of Chief Minister M.K.Stalin taking a holiday in Kodaikkanal to the comparative performance of Tamil Nadu with other southern States with respect to the IT sector to the possibility of a coalition regime getting formed due to the presence of the Vijay-led TVK

title: Ontavantha Pidariyum Oor Pidariyum: The story behind the proverb
content: The proverb’s origins are traced to Udayalur in Thanjavur district, according to noted epigraphist T.N. Subramanian

title: West Bengal Assembly polls Phase 2 LIVE: State records over 61% turnout till 1 p.m. amid sporadic violence
content: According to the Election Commission of India (ECI), Hooghly topped the districts with a turnout of 64.57%, followed by Howrah at 60.68%

title: How Tamil Nadu waited for Assembly election results over the decades
content: For the first time in Tamil Nadu’s history, the Electronic Voting Machines (EVMs) were deployed in all the 234 Assembly segments in the 2001 Assembly elections, mirroring simultaneous rollouts in Kerala, Puducherry, and West Bengal

title: U.S. returns 657 stolen artefacts to India in $14 million restitution
content: Sources said the 657 antiquities were delivered in three phases: 612 were returned in November 2024, 26 in July 2025, and in the third phase, 19 were returned to India on Tuesday (April 28, 2026).

title: Visakha Express rape case: railway worker booked
content: Government Railway Police take over case from Rentachintala police; CCTV footage from Visakhapatnam, Samalkot and Vijayawada stations being examined

title: Month-long Kochi Comedy Festival brings together national and regional artistes
content: The inaugural edition of the Kochi Comedy Festival kicked off on April 19 with Opening Night Show featuring artistes such as Kenny Sebastian, Abish Mathew, Gautham Govindan, Shamik Chakrabarti and Anand Rathnam along with a few artistes from Kerala

title: The Hindu launches tabloid ‘TeenDigest’ for young readers
content: A revamp of The Hindu In School, the 32-page edition weekend tabloid aims to engage curious young minds in an age of endless scrolling. Content ranges from deep dives into science and world affairs, to stories designed to spark creativity.

title: Jharkhand Police arrest prime accused in West Singhbhum treasury-fraud case
content: In the wake of the scam, Jharkhand Finance Minister Radha Krishna Kishore had ordered a comprehensive audit of all district treasuries

title: Ganga Expressway: All you need to know about the expandable six-lane greenfield corridor
content: The Ganga Expressway is a 594-km-long, access-controlled greenfield corridor with six lanes, expandable to eight, and a design speed of 120 kmph

title: Umar Khalid is in jail to ensure society doesn't turn into prison: Prakash Raj at Bengaluru event
content: The event focused on Mr. Khalid’s writings, incarceration, and the broader political and social context reflected in the book

title: PM Modi inaugurates 594-km Ganga Expressway connecting 12 districts from Meerut to Prayagraj
content: CM Adityanath described the six-lane (expandable to eight) expressway as a "lifeline" connecting villages, farmers, entrepreneurs and youth

title: Lokesh sworn in as TDP national working president; Naidu hits out at YSRCP, calls for unity in alliance
content: Chief Minister N. Chandrababu Naidu administers oath to son Nara Lokesh and 252 members of the new TDP committees at NTR Bhavan; says new committees give proper representation to women

title: Mumbai to face 10% water cut from May 15
content: “It is to ensure that Mumbai’s water supply remains in good condition, considering the El Niño effect,” BMC Commissioner Ashwini Bhide said

title: Tamil Nadu government cannot restrict maternity leave to just 12 weeks for third pregnancy: Madras High Court
content: Justices R. Suresh Kumar and N. Senthil Kumar say the pain undergone by women employees for all pregnancies are the same and hence they are entitled to 365 days of maternity leave for third pregnancy too

title: Six foreigners held for prostitution near Madiwala check post in Bengaluru
content: Upon questioning, the women reportedly failed to produce valid passports or visa documents, or provide satisfactory details regarding their stay in India

title: Telangana SSC results 2026 on four websites, MeeSeva WhatsApp number
content: Hyderabad Police Commissioner appealed to parents not to burden their children with hopes, to observe their behavior after results

title: Yash’s ‘Toxic’ postponed again: We are aligning global distribution, say makers
content: ‘Toxic: A Fairytale for Grown-Ups, the much-anticipated film starring Yash and directed by Geetu Mohandas, has been pushed back indefinitely. The movie was set to hit the theatres on June 04, 2026

title: Nandyal SP Suneel Sheoran conducts late-night surprise inspections to curb crime in town
content: SP Suneel Sheoran visits Bommalasatram, Railway Station, Nunepalli, Sai Baba Nagar and other localities; tells personnel to keep watch on rowdy-sheeters and intensify night patrols

title: Schools shut in five Mizoram districts as heavy rain, winds lash State
content: Aizawl, Serchchip, Lunglet, Hnahthial, and Lawngtlai districts shut schools as a precautionary safety measure, School Education Director Angela Zothanpuii said

title: PM Modi holds 14-km roadshow in Varanasi, offers prayers at Kashi Vishwanath temple
content: Devotees and supporters were seen raising slogans such as "Har Har Mahadev" and "Jai Shri Ram" to welcome the Prime Minister

title: BJP will form government in Bengal with thumping majority: BJP chief Nabin
content: “The BJP will form the government in Bengal. We are winning the elections with a massive majority,” Nitin Nabin said

title: West Bengal goes to the polls in the second phase of Assembly election
content: The 142 seats going to the polls in the second phase are spread across seven districts of south Bengal, including Kolkata; about 3.21 crore electors will determine the fate of 1,448 candidates in the fray

title: Hark back to the homes of the royals of Royapettah
content: Besides Amir Mahal, Royapettah has several royal residences, some of which have survived the test of time

title: India, U.K. need to simplify their regulatory mechanisms: London’s Lady Mayor
content: During her maiden visit to India, Mayor Dame Susan Langley emphasised on the need for both the countries to understand each other’s markets more strategically

title: Andhra Pradesh SSC results to be declared on April 30 at 11 a.m., says Lokesh
content: Minister Nara Lokesh says students can check results on the official portal, the Mana Mitra WhatsApp service, the LEAP app and DigiLocker; headmasters can download school-wide results through their logins

title: Congress accuses Rajnath Singh of giving 'clean chit to Pakistan', dubs his remarks at SCO 'anti-national'
content: Jairam Ramesh also alleged that this "new stance" in relation to Pakistan is all part of Prime Minister Narendra Modi's policy of "appeasement" of the U.S.

title: Free health camp in Jambagi village in Vijayapura district by B. M. Patil Medical College Hospital and Research Centre
content: The health camp was held in the Government Higher Primary School in Jambagi H. village of Babaleshwar taluk

title: Security forces, Maoists exchange fire in Jharkhand's West Singhbhum
content: Misir Besra — a politburo member of CPI (Maoist) — remains active in the area along with his group, according to police sources

title: Anant Ambani urges Colombia to halt culling of Escobar’s hippos, offers to bring all 80 of them to Vantara
content: Pablo Escobar brought the hippos from Africa in the 1980s, but after his death, the hippos from his private zoo made a new life in the Magdalena River — where they have attacked fishermen, prompting moves to cull them

title: NDPS court in Mumbai criticises NCB over freeze order; allows actor Rhea Chakraborty to operate account
content: Additional Sessions Judge U.C. Deshmukh passed the order while allowing the application filed by Rhea Chakraborty and her mother Sandhya

title: At least four killed, 20 injured as bus, truck collide in Odisha
content: The accident took place on Tuesday (April 28, 2026) night in Chandiposh police station area when the bus was travelling from Rourkela to Mangalpur in Jajpur district

title: Supreme Court says enforcement of existing laws, not new laws, needed to combat hate speech and crimes
content: The court said hate crimes continue to shed blood because of poor enforcement of existing laws, and not because there are no laws to address the issue and punish the perpetrators

title: Fire breaks out in Ghaziabad high-rise; several residents rescued
content: The blaze erupted on the eleventh floor of the Gaur Green Avenue Society at around 9 a.m., spreading to multiple flats across several floors, a fire department official said

title: Students trying to sell IPL match tickets in black among 24 arrested in Bengaluru
content: The police said that they have recovered as many as 36 tickets from the accused, who are private firm employees and students attempting to sell the tickets at higher prices

title: Hike in milk price necessary, will be implemented: Milma
content: Quantum of increase to be decided after a meeting of Milma board, says K.S. Mani, chairman of Kerala Cooperative Milk Marketing Federation

title: Delhi Excise case: AAP leader Durgesh Pathak says he won’t appear for proceedings
content: Mr. Pathak, like AAP chief Arvind Kejriwal and Manish Sisodia, has written a letter to Delhi High Court Justice Swarna Kanta Sharma saying that he would not appear in person in the matter

title: Gyan Bharatam Mission: 30,996 manuscripts documented in Mysuru district so far
content: The Gyan Bharatam Mission aims to build a national repository of centuries-old handwritten texts and records

title: CITU petitions ILO over ‘suppression of workers’ rights by Centre and Uttar Pradesh governments’
content: ‘Government of India and the Uttar Pradesh Government are in violation of fundamental ILO principles’

title: Mamata accuses BJP of trying to ‘rig’ Bengal elections, alleges central forces acting at its behest
content: Mamata Banerjee claimed that some people from outside the state were trying to create disturbances in the constituency and sought immediate intervention from the Election Commission

title: Madras High Court orders CBI probe into ₹397-crore transformer procurement during Senthilbalaji’s tenure
content: Chief Justice Sushrut Arvind Dharmadhikari and Justice G. Arul Murugan direct the Directorate of Vigilance and Anti Corruption to handover the papers to CBI within two weeks

title: Maharashtra defers action over Marathi rule, to begin training campaign for taxi, auto drivers
content: Transport Minister said while the mandate to speak in Marathi remains in effect, driving licences will not be revoked solely on the grounds of inadequate language proficiency during this period

title: ECI ought to provide voting facilities for all eligible citizens: Kerala HC
content: The ECI contended that many postal ballot applications were either defective or were not received (by the officials).

title: Will AAP MPs face disqualification after joining BJP?
content: Is there a law against defection? What are the exceptions to it and how has the Supreme Court interpreted them? What are experts’ view on seven of AAP’s 10 Rajya Sabha members joining the BJP last week?

title: Trinamool accuses Election Commission’s police observer of serious misconduct
content: Trinamool Congress has accused 2011-batch IPS officer of Uttar Pradesh cadre, also a trained dental surgeon, of intimidating and threatening locals in Falta under West Bengal’s Diamond Harbour constituency.

title: Maharashtra ATS begins probe into Thane knife attack
content: The police say they have recovered handwritten notes allegedly indicating the desire of the accused to join the Islamic State, a banned terrorist organisation

title: EC asks NIA to ensure bombs not used to disrupt Bengal polls on April 29
content: The directive comes after recovery of crude bombs in the poll-bound State and reports of a minor blast

title: Gemini To Meta: How A Deepfake Gang Used AI To Hijack Identities In Gujarat
content: The gang is accused of executing a complex scheme where they acquired victim data and used AI platforms, such as Gemini AI and Meta AI, to generate high-quality deepfake videos featuring "eye-blink" animations.

title: Bengal Elections Phase 2 Voting LIVE: Crude Bombs Found In Purba Bardhaman Amid Polling
content: Bengal Assembly Elections 2026 Phase 2 Voting LIVE Updates: The second phase of voting for the intense BJP vs Trinamool battle will begin shortly.

title: Polling Stopped At Some Bengal Booths After BJP Claims EVM Tampering
content: Amit Malviya, the BJP's media in-charge, has demanded repolling in all affected booths in Falta where such election frauds have occurred.

title: CBI To Probe Rs 397 Crore Contract Issued Under DMK's Senthil Balaji
content: The case originates from a complaint filed by anti-corruption NGO Arappor Iyakkam, which alleged large-scale collusion in the tender process between 2021 and 2023, leading to an estimated loss of Rs 397 crore to the state exchequer.

title: Opinion: Opinion | 'On Verge Of Stopping': Inside The Panic That Led To Indian Airlines' SOS Call
content: The Iran shock has hit every airline in the world. Yet, only three groups have written to governments to keep them flying. One of them comprises the three Indian airlines.

title: After Arvind Kejriwal, Manish Sisodia, Durgesh Pathak To Skip Excise Case Hearing
content: Earlier, Arvind Kejriwal and former deputy chief minister Manish Sisodia had written letters to the High Court after their plea seeking the recusal of Justice Sharma was rejected on April 20.

title: Ukraine And Israel Are Fighting Over 'Stolen' Grains. Russia Is The Reason
content: Hitting out at Kyiv for conducting what he called "Twitter diplomacy", Israeli Foreign Minister Gideon Saar said, if Ukraine has "any evidence of theft, submit it through the appropriate channels."

title: Japan Airlines To Trial Humanoid Robots At Haneda Airport As Labour Shortages Deepen
content: From early May, Japan Airlines will begin a two-year trial in which robots assist baggage handlers on the tarmac.

title: Election-Ready, Always: BJP's New Chief Rolls Out Early 2027 Blueprint
content: BJP insiders say the current push is less about immediate electoral outcomes and more about building a durable political structure.

title: Big Drop In Watermelon Prices In Navi Mumbai After 'Food Poisoning' Deaths
content: With the fruit selling for between Rs 5 to Rs 7 per kilogram, traders are reporting suffering immense financial losses.

title: PETA India, Police Stop Planned Animal Sacrifice At Telangana Temple
content: It further noted that Section 4 bars any person from officiating, performing, assisting, participating or offering to participate in such sacrifices.

title: 6 Lanes, 600 km, 6 Hours: Ganga Expressway, Where You Can Drive At 120 kmph
content: Built at Rs 36,230 crore,Ganga Expressway is Uttar Pradesh's longest expressway and one of India's biggest greenfield road projects.

title: Exit Polls Today: When And Where To Watch As NDTV Decodes Voter Trends
content: NDTV brings you the latest updates on exit polls after voting concludes in four states and one Union territory today.

title: Dr Reddy's Wins Nod to Sell Generic Ozempic, Check Cost Of Weight-Loss Drug
content: The entry of Dr Reddy's generic is triggering a price battle among pharmaceutical companies worldwide.

title: The Saudi-Pakistan Factor Behind UAE's Exit From OPEC
content: The UAE has also been frustrated with Pakistan, partly because of its deepening ties with Saudi Arabia, but also because of what it considers Islamabad's meek role as a mediator between the US and Iran.

title: Trump Tells Aides To Prep For Lengthy Hormuz Blockade: Report
content: The US has been blocking ships going to and from Iranian ports to try to squeeze the country of oil revenue, while Iran keeps the strait closed to almost all other traffic.
